'Freedom Convoy' organizers discussed playing 'race card' with Métis heritage   Organizers of the “Freedom Convoy” discussed using their ties to Métis identity to play the “race card” as part of an overall strategy to control their public image and garner sympathy for their cause, text messages suggest.
Interac services 'fully available' again after Rogers outage
Ottawa sex-trade worker jailed for role in 2018 killing of pimp
Mississippi Mills asking judge to declare homebuilder a 'vexatious' litigant in decade-long dispute over industrial land
'Long, hard summer' for Ontario hospitals amid shutdowns, staffing shortages and growing concern
Rider dies in motorcycle crash in Lanark Highlands Township   It was the second motorcyclist fatality in eastern Ontario since Thursday.

Teen commended for quick actions to save sibling, call 911 for fire   Fire investigators are congratulating a quick-thinking 13-year-old for getting a sibling to safety and quickly calling 911 after the teen spotted black smoke coming from their home’s garage.
Driver dies in single-car crash on eastbound Hwy 174   One person was killed in a single-vehicle crash early Saturday on Highway 174 in the city’s east end.
'Freedom Convoy' organizer Tamara Lich denied bail   “Ms. Lich’s involvement, in this court’s opinion, continues to pose a risk for the protection and safety of the public.”
One dead, one injured in single-vehicle crash near S. Stormont   Ontario Provincial Police continue to investigate a fatal car crash on Highway 401 on Saturday morning.

Pellerin: LRT inquiry reveals a deep chasm in trust between the City of Ottawa and the tax-paying public   What are we supposed to think when those tasked with building critical public infrastructure don’t bother to communicate with us via our elected representatives about serious and significant problems?

Johnson: Intimate-partner violence — misogyny is built in to both our culture and our justice system   All the passes given to rapists, batterers and stalkers, all the obstacles to safety thrown in women’s paths — they are not happening by chance, but by design.
Today's letters: The frustrations of the ArriveCAN app; LRT blues; and protesters   Saturday, July 9: You can write to us too, at letters@ottawacitizen.com

Frenkel: As a Jewish student in Ottawa, I experienced antisemitism first-hand   There were death threats and physical intimidation, much of it veiled in hatred of Israel, but all of it directed at me.
Editorial: Quebec's COVID plan is everyone for themselves, it seems   The situation is "under control," Health Minister Christian Dubé indicated on Thursday. Is it really? And if so, for how long?
Tablu's second-half goal earns Atletico a draw with Cavalry   True to form, there was little space to work with Saturday at TD Place Stadium.

QB OR NOT QB? While waiting for news on injured Masoli, Redblacks need to move on   The coming hours and days could get even more challenging for the winless Ottawa Redblacks, who could be staring at a lengthy absence for starting quarterback Jeremiah Masoli.

GARRIOCH: Even after draft, plenty of 'to do' items for Senators   Even as development camp starts Monday, GM Pierre Dorion and Co. will be working the phones, too, to prepare for the Wednesday start of NHL free agency.
GARRIOCH: A look at the nine prospects the Senators selected in the 2022 NHL draft   Eight of them will arrive in town Sunday.
BEHAR TEES OFF: Redblacks angry after QB Masoli knocked out of loss to Saskatchewan   REGINA – It went from bad to worse Friday night for the Ottawa Redblacks.

'Sopranos' actor Tony Sirico — known for role as Paulie Walnuts — dies at age 79   'A larger than life character on and off screen. Gonna miss you a lot my friend,' Sopranos co-star Steven Van Zandt said

Jazz pianist sensation Marcus Roberts set for midweek show at Music and Beyond festival   Roughly four decades ago and a few years before he burst onto the global jazz scene, Marcus Roberts, then a music student at Florida State University, asked Count Basie how he had become such an exceptional and towering musician.

'I had to do everything extra well just to prove that I know what I’m doing' — Canadian Lara Wong on standing out in the flamenco world   Two decades ago, Lara Wong heard the music that would change her life.

Bluesfest draws crowd of around 20,000 on idyllic opening night   Vancouver pop singer Jessia opened on the main stage, before headliner Sarah McLachlan delivered a hit-packed set with her terrific band
First in-person festival since 2019 is like 'redemption' for Bluesfest director Mark Monahan   Sales have been going strong for this year’s festival, the first since 2019.

Dining Out: Stolen Goods Cocktail Bar's mixologists raise Ottawa's beverage game   Stolen Goods Cocktail Bar 106 Sparks St., stolengoodscocktailbar.com Open: Tuesday to Sunday 6 p.m. to 2 a.m., closed Monday Prices: small plates $10 to $26, full menu $140, cocktails $14.50 to $24; discounted oysters and drinks after 11 p.m. Access: No steps to front door or washrooms

Quebec health officials say there are 248 confirmed monkeypox cases in the province   MONTREAL — Quebec health officials are reporting a dozen new cases of monkeypox for a total of 248 since the outbreak began in the province.

Hospital 'nightmare' in B.C. for Quebec patient denied surgery: father   VANCOUVER — A Quebec man who fell and broke his jaw, cheekbone and a bone around his left eye while visiting British Columbia says his surgery was cancelled after he was told his home province “won’t pay” for the procedure.
Are 4-day workweeks, flexible hours the future of full-time?   A four-day workweek sounds appealing to workers. Possibly alarming to employers.

Ontario likely in new COVID wave driven by BA.5 subvariant, science table says   TORONTO — Ontario is likely in another wave of COVID-19 driven by the highly infectious BA.5 Omicron subvariant of the virus, Ontario’s expert pandemic advisers say.
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

Microsoft's Activision Blizzard bid faces UK antitrust probe   LONDON (AP) — Microsoft’s acquisition of game publisher Activision Blizzard faces antitrust scrutiny in the U.K., where competition regulators said Wednesday they’ve opened an initial inquiry into the $69 billion deal.

Canadian governments OK settlement with Purdue Pharma over opioid addictions   Purdue's actions contributed to the opioid epidemic 'through their deceptive marketing,' says B.C. Attorney General David Eby
Howard Levitt: The Kafkaesque dystopia of workplace investigations in Canada   As an employee, you really do not have any rights in a workplace investigation
Ernst & Young pays US$100 million SEC fine over CPA ethics exam cheaters   The SEC announced on Tuesday that EY would pay a US$100 million fine — the largest ever penalty for an audit firm

Nissan recalls about 323K SUVs; hoods can open unexpectedly   WASHINGTON (AP) — Nissan is recalling nearly 323,000 Pathfinder SUVs in the U.S. because the hoods can unexpectedly fly open and block the driver’s view.
News Roundup: A Canadian bomb-proof Toyota, and a Porsche-powered VW van   Plus: video footage of a 15-year-old off-roader flipping a Jeep off a cliff—don't worry, it ends well
I'd rather watch Nissans race than Ferraris—here's why   The Nissan Sentra Cup was one of the highlights of the Canadian Grand Prix weekend in Montreal, and it's for a good reason

Collector Classics: 1962 Chevrolet Corvette convertible   It was a leap of faith that couples sometimes need to make. He saw a big withdrawal from their joint savings account. She would only say it was a good investment and he needed to trust her judgment. She added that their financial advisor was in favour of the expense.
2023 Toyota Crown teaser hints at sedan-style SUV   The patent images being passed around now seem legit, with the new SUV to be properly revealed on July 15
GMC announces 2023 Sierra 1500 AT4X AEV Edition   Factory-installed heavy-duty parts will make it the most capable off-road Sierra yet

'ALS will be a treatable chronic disease': New treatment slows disease progression and increases life expectancy   McGill University's Dr. Angela Genge is optimistic about what the future holds for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis care: "Every step gives our patients more time, better function for longer, better care every year."
Asking For A Friend: Are essential oils worth the hype?   The oils from plants offer natural healing and cleansing benefits, but they can also be dangerous.
Our friends may be our friends because of the way they smell   When it comes to judging other people on their body odour, we may be more like other mammals than we realize, according to a new study.
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.
Ontario police seize cannabis worth $61 million   Estimated 45,000-plus illegal pot plants and eight metric tonnes of processed weed confiscated.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.
